name: foremost
title: foremost
summary: forensic program to recover lost files
description:
forensic program to recover lost files
Foremost is a forensic program to recover lost files based on
their headers, footers, and internal data structures.
.
Foremost can work on image files, such as those generated by dd,
Safeback, Encase, etc, or directly on a drive. The headers and
footers can be specified by a configuration file or you can use
command line switches to specify built-in file types. These built-in
types look at the data structures of a given file format allowing
for a more reliable and faster recovery.

commands.name: foremost
commands.description: Recover files using their headers, footers, and data structures

arguments.description: Enables write all headers, perform no error detection in terms of corrupted files.
arguments.raw: -a  Enables write all headers, perform no error detection in terms of corrupted files.

arguments.description: Turn on indirect block detection, this works well for Unix file systems.
arguments.raw: -d  Turn on indirect block detection, this works well for Unix file systems.

arguments.description: Show a help screen and exit.
arguments.raw: -h  Show a help screen and exit.

arguments.description: Enables quick mode. In quick mode, only the start of each sector is searched for match-
arguments.raw: -q  Enables quick mode. In quick mode, only the start of each sector is searched for match-

arguments.description: Time stamp the output directory so you don't have to delete the output dir when running
arguments.raw: -T  Time stamp the output directory so you don't have to delete the output dir when running

arguments.description: verbose mode. This causes more information regarding the current state of the
arguments.raw: -v  verbose mode. This causes more information regarding the current state of the

arguments.description: Enables write audit only mode. No files will be extracted.
arguments.raw: -w  Enables write audit only mode. No files will be extracted.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use foremost because forensic program to recover lost files.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use foremost as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `foremost  [-h]  [-V]  [-d]  [-vqwQT]  [-b  <blocksize>]  [-o <dir>] [-t <type>] [-s <num>] [-i` as documented upstream for foremost.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `foremost -s 100 -t jpg -i image.dd` as documented upstream for foremost.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `foremost -av image.dd` as documented upstream for foremost.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `foremost -t all -i image.dd` as documented upstream for foremost.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair foremost with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `foremost --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: foremost - Recover files using their headers, footers, and data structures
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install foremost (1.5.7-11). Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `foremost  [-h]  [-V]  [-d]  [-vqwQT]  [-b  <blocksize>]  [-o <dir>] [-t <type>] [-s <num>] [-i`. Further invocations from upstream: `foremost -s 100 -t jpg -i image.dd`; `foremost -av image.dd`.
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: forensic program to recover lost files.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: foremost - Recover files using their headers, footers, and data structures.
